metadata.title: B2 Key Word Transformations – Complete Dataset
metadata.description: Complete FCE Part 4 dataset: 160 sequential questions + 143 grammar-rule questions across 11 categories

items.sentence: You've broken my radio, Frank! Said Jane.
My car really needs to be repaired soon.

I could never have succeeded without your help.
I thought I might run out of cash, so I took my cash card.
items.pre: I took my cash card with me

items.sentence: They are letting David out of hospital next week.

items.post: you my bike if you're in a hurry.

items.hint: it is rare for + object + infinitive

items.answer: seem to have run out of

You're going too fast for me.

items.sentence: I was really pleased that at last my father had managed to give up smoking.
items.pre: I was really pleased that at last my father

items.post: as the train left the station.

items.post: detail of what happened that day.

items.answer: cannot read the newspaper without wearing

items.answer: thought it unlikely that Peter would

items.post: she learnt to speak English so well.

items.post: Dan is very keen on boats.

items.sentence: I am totally convinced that our team will win.

'Do not answer the phone, Rebecca!' said Mrs Miniver.

My car was so badly damaged that I didn't repair it.
items.pre: My car was so badly damaged that

This bottle is completely empty.
My uncle did not learn to drive until he was forty.
I haven't seen my brother since he left for Australia.
items.post: my brother was before he left for Australia.

items.sentence: The price of the holiday includes the cost of insurance.

items.pre: Tom criticised his son for having

items.sentence: You ought to get your bicycle brakes repaired immediately.

items.hint: it is not like + object + infinitive

items.sentence: I would prefer it if nobody else knew what happened last night.

The taxi may be expensive so take plenty of money.

I didn't know it was your birthday, so I didn't send you a card.
items.pre: If I'd known it was your birthday, I

I've had enough of your untidiness.
Can I borrow your surfboard?
My parents didn't let me stay out late when I was younger.
items.post: out late when I was younger.

items.sentence: How long was your flight from Frankfurt to Seoul?
The last time I went skiing was ten years ago.

items.post: to afford such an expensive holiday.

items.sentence: Teenagers aren't buying as many pop records these days.
I regret inviting Nancy to the party.

metadata.title: Key Word Transformations – Grammar Rules 2026
metadata.description: 143 key word transformation sentences organised by grammar category – Cambridge FCE Part 4

items.sent: George was late, so we didn't go to the cinema.
items.post: we would have gone to the cinema.

items.sent: Jim missed the game because he was ill.
items.post: he wouldn't have missed the game.

items.sent: Tracy broke her arm because she was not cautious.
items.post: she wouldn't have broken her arm.

items.sent: Due to his careless driving, there was a terrible accident.
items.post: there wouldn't have been a terrible accident.

items.sent: The team lost the game because they played badly.
He always overcooks the rice!
The bank is unpopular because they charge so much.
items.pre: They would be more popular if they

items.sent: What a pity I have to go to work today.
This train journey seems endless.
In the snowy weather we don't go to school.
What a pity we all ate the same food.
I don't like being tall.
Unless Peter repairs the roof, he is unlikely to find a buyer.
items.pre: Peter would not find a buyer

items.sent: People are eating most of the food at the party.
They think that the reporter had stolen the document.
They ought to have organised annual parties for migrants.
items.blank: ought to have been organised for migrants by

items.sent: The workmen are fixing our pool.
The porter didn't deliver our suitcase.
She might have sent us the wrong parcel.
items.blank: might have been sent the wrong parcel by

items.sent: Nobody knows anything about Sam.
The manager made all the plans for the team.
They regularly make annual reports.
Everyone knows that a foreign company will complete the project for us.
items.blank: will have the project completed by a foreign

items.sent: The firm is running computer courses for elderly people.
items.blank: are being run for elderly people by the firm

items.sent: They think the company is facing new challenges.
They are not doing enough to help the economy.
"The child must repeat the test!", said the nurse.
items.blank: that the child had to repeat

items.sent: "No-one should be blamed for the accident except the driver," said Mr Jones.
"Why don't we just apply for the licence instead?" said Mr Lewis.
items.blank: suggested that they should just apply

items.sent: "I don't think Mr Jones will consider my proposal."
The teenager said she did not break the vase.
"Where can I find this shop, please?"
"All right, it's true. I was nervous," said Jack.
"I am not sure if she is making the right decision."
"Please don't share this secret with anyone."
"I will tell your parents if you keep coming late," said Mrs Adams.
"It wasn't me who stole your money."
items.blank: it being his fault for stealing

items.sent: "No, sorry, I don't want to lend you my camera."
"No matter what, you must always keep this door shut."
I am sure Alan took the parcel by mistake.
It is possible he left his keys in the office.
I am absolutely certain he didn't take your watch.
It was not necessary to take your car.
It was a mistake to buy that car.
Perhaps Alan didn't mean what he said.
We could never have known the truth.
items.blank: was impossible for us to have known

items.sent: It is likely that she wore the wrong dress.
Without your help, the outcome would be different.

You should have taken my advice.

I usually find it difficult to sleep on the floor.

Trying to speak to you is a complete waste of time.

I don't think Derek remembered his appointment this morning.

John forbade his children from leaving the house.
Williams did not look as if he had noticed the problem.
I shouldn't have moved to this part of the country.
items.post: to this part of the country.
